what_it_does: This file is a design-system manifest for the ConceptVDesignSystem namespace: it catalogs UI and core components, grouped preview cards, deck templates, global CSS files, a large set of CSS custom-property tokens, theme/density/surface selectors, and brand fonts.
observations: Lists 11 UI components and 6 core components; RenderType/CoreTypes share core/RenderType.jsx and Slide/Archetypes share core/Slide.jsx.
Contains 56 preview cards grouped by AI, Axis System, Brand, Colors, Components, Glyphic System, Spacing, Type System, and UI Kits, each with viewport size and subtitle.
Defines 2 templates (Capital Raise, Pitch Deck) with captured thumbnails and generative-core descriptions.
Includes a long flat tokens array spanning colors, shadows, spacing, fonts, motion, depth, layout, states, focus, icons, dataviz, imagery, canvas, provenance, export, texture, and containers.
Tokens use semantic pigment names (pig-*) and zone names (zone-*) with color-mix and intensity variables.
Themes cover dim, dark, contrast, clean/warm ground, desktop/tablet/mobile-portrait surfaces, and compact/comfortable/spacious density.
Brand fonts Sora, DM Sans, and JetBrains Mono are mapped to token usages.
startingPoints is empty and hasThumbnailHtml is false despite templates listing thumbnails.
standouts: A token system built on semantic zones (base, content, panel, review, source, package, success, warning, reject) mixed from pigments and a ground color.
Scoped token overrides for theme, density, surface, and palette are stored in the same flat list using name repetition and scope fields.
Templates are described as computed from a generative core using content-as-data and LOD/density/theme axis dials.
AI-specific cards and a Vi workspace UI kit suggest the design system is co-designed with an AI agent layer.
Duplicate component names pointing to the same sourcePath imply aliases or bundled exports.
conventions: Manifest uses arrays of typed objects with consistent keys (name, sourcePath, path, group, viewport, etc.).
Tokens record kind, definedIn file, optional scope selector, and optional annotation.
Cards are grouped by functional category and carry explicit viewport dimensions.
Global CSS paths are listed separately from component source paths.
concerns: Several tokens have a kind of color or shadow that does not match their value type (e.g., calc expressions, font stacks, z-index numbers).
The same CSS variable name is declared multiple times with different scopes and values, which may be intentional overrides but is not explicitly marked as such.
Distinct component names map to identical sourcePath entries, making it unclear whether they are aliases or separate named exports.
Some card subtitles are truncated mid-sentence.
startingPoints is empty even though many cards and templates could serve as entry points.
notes: A central catalogue that tries to make every design-system asset—components, previews, tokens, themes, fonts—discoverable in one JSON file.
questions: Are RenderType/CoreTypes and Slide/Archetypes intentional aliases or separate exports from the same file?
How are the scoped token redefinitions resolved (CSS cascade, build-time merge, runtime switch)?
What consumes this manifest—build tooling, a documentation site, or the Vi AI workspace?
What does source:'spa' indicate about the project type?
How do templates compute from the generative core, and what is the content schema?
Why is hasThumbnailHtml false when templates list captured thumbnails?
purpose_vs_actual: No explicit purpose field exists in the file; its structure is consistent with a design-system manifest and token registry.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to create a single machine-readable ledger that lets tooling discover, theme, preview, and generate design-system outputs without parsing individual source files.
novelty: The combination of semantic pigment/zone tokens with intensity multipliers, scoped overrides in a flat token list, generative deck templates, and explicit AI/Vi integration cards in a component manifest.
contribution: It contributes a structured inventory of components, preview cards, templates, global styles, and design tokens that a fused system can use for discovery, build orchestration, documentation generation, and theming.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON manifest for the ConceptVDesignSystem namespace listing UI and core components, grouped preview cards, generative deck templates, global CSS files, an extensive set of scoped CSS custom-property tokens, themes, and brand fonts.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as a central, machine-readable catalogue and token registry for building, theming, and previewing the ConceptV design system.
